rulings.Case Title: KAUSHIK DATTANI VS. CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 8, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY, DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. There is no breach of contract when an insurer exercises its right to settle a claim. There is no breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing when an insurer exercises its express right to settle a claim. All of plaintiff's causes of action are impermissibly founded upon the insurer's right to settlement a claim. No reasonable probability that defects may be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW/pm)

rulings.Case Title: GUY DOWNING et al VS. YANNOULA KOLIATSIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 8, 2009, line 9, PLAINTIFF GUY DOWNING, and, JAMES SNIDLE'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees. Motion for attorneys' fees is granted. Reasonable and necessary attorney fees awarded in the amount of $14,487.94. The terms of the stipulation for entry of judgment control. In view of the ambiguity in Paragraph 22 regarding "reasonable attorney's fees" and "the actual amount of attorney's fees incurred by Plaintiffs," the court admits the extrinsic evidence offered by both parties to interpret the stipulation. The Court finds both parties' emails and particularly plaintiff's counsel's October 3, 2008 email (Exhibit E to Foster Declaration in Support of Further Reply) to be substantial evidence that Pargraph 22 includes all of the fees incurred from the commencement of the case, including those related to the first unlawful detainer action. The unlawful detainer actions were related, based on nonpayment, and constituted a continuous series of events subject to the attorney fee provision in Paragraph 22 of the Stipulation. =(302/CWW/PB)
